_comment: Source 2: SEBI investor article — 'How Does Exit Load Work?' summarized and hardcoded (no scrape) to avoid token waste.
bullets: When you redeem, the fund house calculates exit load from the redemption amount and the time of redemption.
Structures differ by fund type: equity funds often apply exit loads for redemptions within about a year; debt and other categories may use shorter windows or none.
Exit loads discourage frequent trading and help managers keep a more stable pool of assets.
With fewer short-term redemptions, the portfolio can stay more stable for all investors.
Some funds reduce the load over time (e.g. higher if redeemed very early); liquid funds often charge no exit load.
